title: Add regression tests for the machine-readable workflow-guidance contract
summary: Protect the 0.5.4 workflow-guidance improvements with regression coverage so lifecycle-relevant MCP tools continue to expose structured canonical state and structured recovery guidance in a stable, agent-safe form. This ticket ensures that future changes do not regress success responses back to ambiguous minimal payloads or failure responses back to string-only lifecycle errors that force AI agents to guess canonical status, terminality, follow-up obligations, or resume behavior.
outputs: Regression tests that verify lifecycle wrapper success responses include structured canonical workflow guidance
Regression tests that verify helper success responses expose explicit follow-up obligations and terminal/non-terminal state correctly
Regression tests that verify lifecycle and state failures expose structured recovery guidance and stable error-code behavior
Regression tests that verify begin-required, resume-required, terminal, and integrity-blocked cases remain distinguishable by machine-readable fields
acceptance_criteria: Tests verify that lifecycle wrapper tools return structured canonical guidance fields such as canonical status, canonical phase, next action, terminality, follow-up requirements, and active transaction identity.
Tests verify that helper success responses clearly distinguish non-terminal `committed` from terminal `done` and expose required follow-up when terminal closure is still needed.
Tests verify that lifecycle/state failures expose structured recovery guidance instead of forcing clients to rely only on human-readable error strings.
Tests verify that begin-required, resume-required, terminal-transaction, and integrity-blocked cases remain machine-distinguishable and do not collapse into ambiguous generic failures.
Tests verify that active transaction identity and new-ticket/resume guidance remain stable enough for agent branching.
Regression coverage fails if lifecycle-aware tools regress to ambiguous minimal payloads or contradictory canonical guidance.
tasks: Add or update tests for lifecycle wrapper success responses so they assert the presence and consistency of canonical workflow-guidance fields.
Add or update tests for verify/commit helper success responses so they assert follow-up obligations, terminal flags, and non-terminal `committed` semantics.
Add or update tests for structured lifecycle/state failures so they assert stable machine-readable fields such as reason, error code, recommended next action, and active transaction context.
Add tests that distinguish begin-required, resume-required, terminal, and integrity-blocked outcomes without depending only on string matching.
Add assertions that the guidance contract remains internally consistent across tools and does not expose contradictory status, phase, next action, or terminality.
notes: This ticket protects the 0.5.4 response contract rather than introducing new lifecycle semantics by itself.
The most important regression to prevent is a return to tool responses that require hidden implementation knowledge or natural-language inference for correct agent behavior.
Coverage should focus on machine-actionable contract guarantees rather than documentation wording alone.
Tests should preserve strict canonical behavior; they should not encourage weakening lifecycle validation or hiding integrity-blocked states.
